phase7LaunchConditions.gateMeaning: The one-portal shell and accessibility coverage are stable enough for internal and staff-only assistive foundations.
Release controls, route freezes, kill switches, and rollback posture are present enough to define the assistive release law.
Identity and route continuity may be consumed by staff-only shadow and draft-generation tracks.
Artifact presentation and outbound navigation law can govern transcript and draft artifacts.
Telemetry disclosure law supports evaluation, shadow capture, and suggestion-event design.
Support, governance, audit, and what-the-patient-saw traceability are stable enough for early internal operator surfaces.

openPreconditions.title: Human approval remains authoritative
openPreconditions.rule: First-wave work may draft, suggest, replay, and evaluate; it may not commit final clinical action or operational state.

openPreconditions.title: Shadow-first and local non-production posture
openPreconditions.rule: Backend implementations must default to protected evaluation, internal, or shadow surfaces until later rollout contracts open visibility.

openPreconditions.title: No patient-visible AI output
openPreconditions.rule: Generated drafts and suggestion envelopes are staff-side artifacts only; patient-facing publication remains blocked.

openPreconditions.title: Regulatory and safety control starts before promotion
openPreconditions.rule: The release-candidate and regulatory trigger law must be frozen before any model-bearing promotion or live submission.

humanControlBoundaries: Assistive outputs may never become task closure, endpoint decision, appointment record, pharmacy case, or escalation record without final human settlement.
Suggestion insert, regenerate, dismiss, export, and close-task affordances remain blocked until leases and trust envelopes exist.
Feedback eligibility remains pending until final human artifact linkage, downstream settlement, exclusion checks, and revocation law are implemented.
